metadata.name: swampyer
metadata.summary: Simple WAMP library with minimal external dependencies

functions.name: swampyer.serializers.available_serializers
documentation: Returns a list of the current available serializers on the system

methods.name: swampyer.messages.WampMessage.package
documentation:
If the debug is set to True, this will exclude any field marked `hide_from_debug`
from the resulting send package.

methods.name: swampyer.messages.WampMessage.get
documentation:
Works like dict's get. Seeks out the attribute value and
return if found, if not, returns none

methods.name: swampyer.messages.WampMessage.set
documentation: Works like dict's set. An attribute to the provided value

class_variables.name: swampyer.messages.WampMessage._fields
documentation: autoset

class_variables.name: swampyer.messages.WampMessage.code_name
documentation: autoset

class_variables.name: swampyer.messages.WampMessage.serializer
documentation: autoset

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyRunner.start
documentation:
We provide the Runner the queue to throw events against
at the invocation time. It'd be annoying to the programmer
to have to do so at invocation

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyRunner.work
documentation: Override this function!

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyRunner.handle_error
documentation: Triggered for whatever reason when handling this runner fails

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyRunner.run
documentation:
This wraps the run so that we can catch when the
thread finishes

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyEvent.handle_error
documentation: handle_error is only relevant if there's a runner associated

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.reset
documentation:
When we need to expunge all the queues. This may happen
when we're forced to reconnect

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.stats
documentation:
Return the current stats object. Just a simple counter based
report on what the client has been up to. Adds one parameter
`timestamp` which holds the current epoch time

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.put
documentation: Notify the concurrency loop that we'd like to start a thread

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.put_exit
documentation: Notify the concurrency loop that a thread has been finished

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.active_count
documentation: Returns the number of active threads

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.waitlist_count
documentation: Returns the number of waiting threads

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.queue_full
documentation:
Returns True if there is a max concurrency value for the
queue and it happens to have been reached

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.waitlist_full
documentation:
Returns True if the number of jobs waiting surpasses the allowed
value in self.queue_max. If self.queue_max is 0, there's no limit
to the number of jobs allowed to wait

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.queue_empty
documentation:
Returns a true value if both the active and the waiting
queue are empty

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.transfer
documentation:
This takes an existing queue and transfers the queue data over
to this instance. Typically used when replacing classes

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.job_queued
documentation:
Called when an event comes in that exceeds our current queue
limit

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.job_reject
documentation:
Called when an event comes in that exceeds our waitlist
limit and rejected. Once this function is called, the system
will raise the ExWaitlistFull exeception

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.queue_init
documentation: Triggered when a request for a new job is received by the queue

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.queue_exit
documentation: Triggered when a running job completes

methods.name: swampyer.queues.ConcurrencyQueue.queue_event
documentation:
Triggered whenever an event is received on the event queue. Probably not
that useful unless one wishes to manage queues entirely

methods.name: swampyer.transport.Transport.send_message
documentation:
Used by the client to send a message object. This will
allow us to intercept and serialize to the appropriate
format before sending it on.

methods.name: swampyer.transport.WebsocketTransport.next
documentation: Returns the next  buffer element

classes.name: swampyer.transport.RawsocketTransport

methods.name: swampyer.transport.RawsocketTransport.perform_handshake
documentation: Negotiates the serialization format

methods.name: swampyer.transport.RawsocketTransport.next
documentation: Returns the next  buffer element

Raw Socket transport is detailed here:

https://github.com/wamp-proto/wamp-proto/blob/master/rfc/text/advanced/ap_transport_rawsocket.md

classes.name: swampyer.client.WampInvokeWrapper
documentation:
Used to put invoke requests on a separate thread
so we can make WAMP requests while in a WAMP request

classes.name: swampyer.client.WampSubscriptionWrapper
documentation:
Used to put invoke requests on a separate thread
so we can make WAMP requests while in a WAMP request

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.get_full_uri
documentation: Returns the full URI with prefix attached

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.connect
documentation: This just creates the transport

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.heartbeat
documentation:
starts a new thread that sends the transport ping messages to
the router.

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.stats
documentation:
Return the current stats object. Just a simple counter based
report on what the client has been up to. Adds one parameter
`timestamp` which holds the current epoch time

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.is_disconnected
documentation: returns a true value if the connection is currently dead

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.is_connected
documentation: returns a true value if the connection is currently active

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.concurrency_config_get
documentation:
Returns the normalized config for a particular queue
if available

Attributes allowed in the concurrency_configs maybe:

{
    concurrency_max: integer,
    queue_max: integer,
    loop_timeout: float,

    _class: class to use when creating this queue.
            When required the _class will be invoked with
            _class( queue_name, **normalized_config )
}

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.concurrency_queue_create
documentation: Creates a new ConcurrencyQueue instance

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.concurrency_queue_allowed
documentation: Returns a true value if the concurrency queue is allowed

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.concurrency_queue_get
documentation:
Returns the queue identified by `queue_name`. If no queue exists by 
that name (yet) and self.concurrency_strict_naming exists, the
queue will be instantiated and returned

If a queue by the name `queue_name` does not already exist, creates
it with the concurrency_configs value. If concurrency_configs value is a 
simple int, will use that. If it's a dict, will first search for
the `queue_name` and a value and use that if present otherwise looks
for "default" and uses that one instead

If the `queue_name` is `unlimited`, this will put the invocation or
subscription into a queue that runs immediately regardless of the
current default concurrency limit globally set.

If the `queue_name` has not been defined explicitly by the user
at instantiation, it will silently create one but use the system
default queue size

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.concurrency_queue_run
documentation:
Puts a single runnable into the concurrency queue based upon
the name of the queue. If no queue_name is provided, defaults
to 'default'

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_challenge
documentation:
Executed when the server requests additional
authentication

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_connect
documentation: When the transport has initially connected

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.transport_connect
documentation: Attempt to hook up the transport

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.hello
documentation:
Say hello to the server and wait for the welcome
message before proceeding

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.call
documentation: Sends a RPC request to the WAMP server

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.receive_message
documentation:
Receives a message data from the transport and normalizes
it into a WampMessage

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.send_message
documentation:
Send awamp message to the server. We don't wait
for a response here. Just fire out a message

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.send_and_await_response
documentation:
Used by most things. Sends out a request then awaits a response
keyed by the request_id

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.dispatch_to_awaiting
documentation: Send data to the appropriate queues

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_welcome
documentation: Hey cool, we were told we can access the server!

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_result
documentation: Dispatch the result back to the appropriate awaiter

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_goodbye
documentation: Dispatch the result back to the appropriate awaiter

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_subscribed
documentation: Handle the successful subscription

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_registered
documentation: Handle the request registration

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_error
documentation: OOops! An error occurred

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_abort
documentation: We're out?

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_invocation
documentation:
Passes the invocation request to the appropriate
callback.

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_event
documentation: Send the event to the subclass or simply reject

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.handle_unknown
documentation:
We don't know what to do with this. So we'll send it
into the queue just in case someone wants to do something
with it but we'll just blackhole it.

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.subscribe
documentation: Subscribe to a uri for events from a publisher

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.unsubscribe
documentation: Unsubscribe an existing subscription

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.publish
documentation: Publishes a messages to the server

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.disconnect
documentation:
Disconnect from the transport and pause the process
till we reconnect

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.shutdown
documentation:
Request the system to shutdown the main loop and shutdown the system
This is a one-way trip! Reconnecting requires a new connection
to be made!

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.start
documentation: Initialize the transport, say hello, and start listening for events

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.reconnect
documentation:
Attempt to reconnect to the WAMP server. Thia also assumes that
the main loop is still running

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.register
documentation:
Puts a function on the bus.

- uri: ustring URI to put on the bus
- callback: method invoked to respond to any calls made to URI
- details: dict of options
- concurrency_queue: string. By default a queue for each registration is used
    The maximum sizes of the concurrency queues are set the session attribute
    `self.concurrency_map`

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClient.run
documentation:
Waits and receives messages from the server. This
function somewhat needs to block so is executed in its
own thread until self._request_shutdown is called.

methods.name: swampyer.client.WAMPClientTicket.handle_challenge
documentation:
Executed when the server requests additional
authentication
